checks.detail: checked 3 candidate-building modules for ground-truth imports (none should exist); backtest/ground_truth/ and backtest/frozen_worlds/ confirmed directory-separated

Found 3 real market-vs-outcome surprises (>5-spot gap required to pass): Christian Watson (WR): ADP had him at WR129 pre-season, actually finished WR16 (13.24 PPG) -- a 113-spot surprise; Sterling Shepard (WR): ADP had him at WR156 pre-season, actually finished WR70 (6.31 PPG) -- a 86-spot surprise; Parker Washington (WR): ADP had him at WR120 pre-season, actually finished WR36 (10.79 PPG) -- a 84-spot surprise. Large positive surprises are structurally impossible if this world's ADP secretly knew the season's outcome -- their presence is itself evidence against leakage, not proof by absence.
